finding: tool_name_server_map entries are inserted per before_llm_call but only removed in get_accumulated_content, so a stream that errors/aborts before finalize orphans the per-message entry — unbounded map growth over server uptime.

finalize splits the returned bare name on the first '__' before map lookup, so a tool whose bare name itself contains '__' (e.g. get__weather or the empty-prefix __query_rag) is looked up by the wrong substring and fails recovery, and tool_name is corrupted.

the DB used_ids seed uses get_message_with_content which loads+parses ALL blocks (incl. large tool_result blobs) every finalize; because blocks accumulate across iterations this is O(n^2) blob deserialization on the hot path when a tool_use-only query would suffice.

neither integration test exercises the unique-id fix end-to-end: both emit a single 'tool_use' id (unique in its message), so the used_ids DB-seed + minting wiring is guarded only by the unit helper — a regression that stops seeding used_ids would ship uncaught.

on a transient DB error the used_ids seed degrades to within-batch-only dedup; a provider reusing a constant non-empty id across iterations would then keep the colliding id and the resume pass would skip it as already-executed.

Reviewed: the accumulator Mutex is dropped (drained into a local Vec) before any .await, and tool_name_server_map is locked only for read+clone / write, never across await; outer-keying by message_id isolates concurrent branches. No lock-across-await or race found.

Reviewed: server_id recovery only resolves UNAMBIGUOUS names from the per-message set of tools actually advertised to this user this turn, so a model-supplied bare name cannot be mapped to an unintended/inaccessible server; ambiguous/unknown fail closed to a clear error. No dispatch-to-wrong-server or approval bypass.

Reviewed: the change does not alter approval gating — code_sandbox execute_command still routes through ManualApprove; recovery populates server_id but does not mark anything auto-approved or builtin-bypassed. No authz regression.

Reviewed: the new map field/init mirror tool_use_accumulator; the four error branches mirror the existing success-path (executed_tool_use_ids.push + delete_tool_approval) and error-ToolResult construction; the justfile recipe mirrors check-sandbox-unit. Conforms.

Reviewed: minting call_<uuid> and populating recovered server_id are round-trip-safe — ziee owns both the id it persists on the tool_use block and the id the tool_result/approval reference and the id sent back to the provider; well-behaved unique provider ids are preserved unchanged. No downstream contract break.
